mechanism_narrative: Sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.16% as of April 2018 indicates accommodative stance
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payroll employment signals strengthening labor market conditions and rising consumer purchasing power, which drives aggregate demand and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 133,755 thousand as of April 2018 shows sustained employment growth
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following positive PAYEMS growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in retail sales (RSAFS) signals weakening consumer demand and deteriorating economic momentum, triggering equity risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 411,591 million as of April 2018
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following RSAFS declines > 1.5% are positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC > -0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth accelerates above its 12-month trend, signaling robust manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side expansion that supports corporate profit margins and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month growth rate exceeds 12-month average by > 0.5 percentage points
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following INDPRO acceleration signals are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint deterioration in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) signals synchronized weakness across residential and consumer demand channels, indicating broad-based demand destruction that compresses equity risk premiums and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 5% month-over-month AND RSAFS declines > 1% month-over-month in same month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 872 thousand as of March 2018, RSAFS at 411,591 million as of April 2018
regime_hint: synchronized demand shock or recession onset
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following joint HOUST and RSAFS declines are positive in > 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC > -0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises above 4.5% while nonfarm payroll growth turns negative, signaling labor market deterioration and rising recession risk that triggers equity sell-offs and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 4.5% AND PAYEMS declines month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 7.9% as of April 2018 (note: this appears to be data error; typical 2018 UNRATE was 3.7-4.0%)
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early recession signal
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following joint UNRATE rise and PAYEMS decline are positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC > -0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 2%, signaling real rate compression that forces the Fed into a tightening cycle, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as investors anticipate rate hikes and multiple compression.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 231.75 as of April 2018, FEDFUNDS at 0.16% as of April 2018
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following real rate compression signals are positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC > -0.01 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates below 2% annualized rate while housing starts (HOUST) decline, signaling demand-side weakness and potential recession entry that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as equity valuations compress on lower growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth < 2.0% AND HOUST declines > 3% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13,616.2 billion as of Q1 2018, HOUST at 872 thousand as of March 2018
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following joint GDP deceleration and housing decline are positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC > -0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.
